mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a robust, scalable solution without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM Cloud for management and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
The IBM Cloud CLI is required to interact with IBM Cloud services. This command installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in IBM Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --num-workers 2 --kube-version 1.30

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command sets the context for kubectl to interact with your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-autoscale-enable --cluster my-cluster

ibmcloud ks cluster-monitoring-enable --cluster my-cluster

```

> Enables autoscaling for your cluster.
> Enables monitoring for your cluster.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to bypass confirmation prompts.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are cleaned up.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete my-cluster --force

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.31

```

> Replace '1.31' with the desired version.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the nodes after the upgrade to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Ensure all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Verification
upgrade.description:
Verify that all applications are functioning as expected after the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check that all pods are running without issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to set the correct context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Check your cluster's resource allocation and consider scaling up the nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens when there are not enough resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and consider adding more worker nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the user does not have the necessary permissions to perform the action.

**Fix:**
Ensure your IBM Cloud account has the correct permissions or roles assigned.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity admission and improved scheduling capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs that are no longer recommended for use.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the new features and adjust your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed before proceeding.
